text.title: Idea Generation Frameworks
text.summary: Four bundled methods: 7P framework, Paul Graham diagnostic questions, pain-point mining techniques, and their sequencing as a pipeline.

section.title: 7P Framework
section.p: Seven lenses for generating candidate ideas. For each P, generate 3-5 ideas and score them on Market size (1-5) + Personal fit (1-5) + Urgency (1-5). Top 3 scores proceed to niche scoring.

list.item: Pain: What frustrates you daily? List 5 annoyances, rate severity 1-10.
Passion: What would you do for free? Activities and hobbies with sustainable energy.
Profession: What is broken in your industry? Insider knowledge of inefficiencies.
Process: What workflow takes 10x longer than it should?
Platform: What is missing from an existing platform? Missing integrations, poor UX.
People: What do your network contacts complain about repeatedly?
Product: What product would you pay for immediately if it existed?

section.title: Paul Graham Questions
section.p: Four diagnostic questions for surfacing hidden opportunities:

list.item: What is tedious but necessary? High-frequency, low-satisfaction tasks everyone must do.
What is surprisingly hard to do? Things that should be simple but require workarounds.
What do you find yourself building for yourself? Personal tools that could scale.
What would you pay for that does not exist? Direct willingness-to-pay signal.

rule.statement: Time-box each PG question to 30 minutes of stream-of-consciousness writing. No editing during generation; convergence comes in the scoring phase.
rule.rationale: Editing during ideation activates the critic and suppresses unconventional candidates. Unfiltered output then convergence produces broader coverage than filtered ideation.

section.title: Pain-Point Mining
section.p: Four techniques for surfacing normalized pains you may have stopped noticing:

list.item: Complaint audit: log every complaint for one week, note frequency and intensity, categorize by work/personal/tools.
Workaround inventory: list spreadsheets, scripts, and processes you maintain to work around broken tools.
Tool stack analysis: list all tools you use, rate satisfaction 1-5, identify inter-tool gaps.
Time tracker: track where time goes for 3 days, identify recurring time sinks, calculate cost of inefficiency.

section.p: Scoring weights for pain mining output: Frequency (30%), Intensity (30%), Current solutions (20%), Your ability to solve (20%).

section.title: Pipeline sequencing

rule.statement: Run the four methods in sequence: (1) divergent ideation via 7P + PG, (2) pain audit to refine candidates, (3) niche scoring to filter top 3. Do not run all in parallel — they build on each other.
rule.rationale: Running all methods simultaneously generates overlapping, unsequenced output that is harder to deduplicate and filter. Sequential execution each feeds refined input to the next stage.

section.reference: Per-P idea capture and scoring rows
